Container.Name: curatorx
Container.Overview: Chat-first Plex collection curator with BYOP LLM support, RAG over your library, TMDB discovery, and confirmation-gated Radarr/Sonarr add/remove. Keep port 8788 on a trusted LAN — do not WAN-expose without an authenticated reverse proxy.
Container.Requires: Plex Media Server on your LAN. Optional: Radarr, Sonarr, Tautulli, Fanart.tv, and an LLM API key or Ollama on the Unraid host.
Container.Changes:
### 1.7.11 - Fix owner dashboard data retrieval: 6 frontend/backend field-name mismatches causing empty panels - Decade Distribution, Top Genres, Countries, Languages, Runtime Distribution, Purge Candidates, TV Completion, and Reviews panels now populate correctly
### 1.7.10 - Fix Unraid Force Update: build timestamp now embedded in image file content, not just labels - Startup log shows build info for version verification
### 1.7.9 - Value-based test deep dive: 193 new logic-level tests, shared conftest.py infrastructure, coverage from 1.4% to 76%+
### 1.7.8 - Fix scroll chip, turnstyle buttons, agent tool data consistency; 33 new validation tests
### 1.7.7 - Fix Docker layer caching — Force Update now pulls fresh images reliably
### 1.7.6 - Fix wrong titles in Turnstyle viewport; fix streaming monologue leak
### 1.7.5 - Fix persona selector not showing on upgraded installs
### 1.7.4 - Wire persona selector into chat composer; complete all 7 personality sliders; sanitize remaining error responses
### 1.7.3 - Fix container startup on existing installs with root-owned config
### 1.7.2 - Fix scheduler heartbeat deadline bug; zero test failures
### 1.7.1 - Scheduler circuit breaker: quarantines tasks after 3 consecutive failures (1h cooldown, admin reset endpoint) - Data retention pruning for telemetry/anniversaries with automatic VACUUM - Trickle ingestion caps semantic embedding work per scheduler cycle (MAX_ITEMS_PER_CYCLE=50) - Agent/scheduler boundary and SQLite concurrency model documented - 38 new tests, 0 regressions
### 1.7.0 - Non-root Docker container (security S13) — runs as UID/GID 1000 - True token-by-token SSE streaming for OpenAI + Anthropic LLM providers - Local-password authentication (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256, owner-only registration) - OIDC authentication for homelab IdPs (Authelia, Authentik, Keycloak) - Multi-method login page (Plex + local + OIDC, dynamically configured) - Telemetry ingestion module with admin API endpoints - 48 new tests, 0 regressions
### 1.6.0 - Owner Dashboard: library composition charts, health gauges, storage intelligence, taste timeline - Background idle task scheduler with 6 OOTB tasks (embeddings, taste refresh, health, anniversaries, recs, gaps) - Chat delight: On This Day, Library at a Glance, Night Owl runtime picks, Double Feature, Surprise Me - Genre filter bugfix: comma-separated genres now OR instead of AND
### 1.5.0 - Per-conversation persona selection with 5 built-in + custom personas - 7 personality sliders (4 new: depth, obscurity, verbosity, formality) - Composer persona dropdown with create/edit and Show Advanced toggle - Login page cleanup and privacy link fixes - Test coverage: 64 new tests, coverage tooling, CI pipeline updated
### 1.4.0 - Security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, XSSTO) on all responses - Error response sanitization — no internal details leak to clients - Rate limiting on chat endpoints (30 req/min) - System config endpoint gated behind owner auth - Roadmap: purged ML clustering; added semantic plot embeddings - MCP design philosophy documentation
### 1.3.0 — Initial CA template - Image line: `romwil/curatorx:1.5` (pin `:1.4.0` if you need an exact build) - Config volume `/config` for settings.json + SQLite - Dual MCP keys: privacy (`CURATORX_MCP_API_KEY`) and full (`CURATORX_MCP_FULL_API_KEY`); or generate in Admin → Advanced - Optional seed envs for Plex / *arr / TMDB / LLM; session + webhook secrets for multi-user and Plex webhooks
Container.Description:
CuratorX helps you curate your movie and TV collection through conversation. Connect Plex, Radarr, Sonarr, TMDB, and your preferred LLM provider (cloud API or Ollama).
**Security** - Bind to a trusted LAN or put CuratorX behind an authenticated reverse proxy. - Do **not** port-forward bare `:8788` to the internet. - Optional multi-user: set `CURATORX_SESSION_SECRET` (or accept the auto-generated secret under Config). - Plex webhooks require `CURATORX_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. - Optional MCP at `/mcp`: set privacy and/or full API keys (they must differ), or generate them in **Admin → Advanced**.
**First run** 1. Open the Web UI at port **8788**. 2. Complete setup in **Settings** / **Admin** (Plex server URL + token, TMDB, LLM; optionally Radarr/Sonarr). 3. Map movie and TV libraries, then run **Sync library**. 4. Start chatting with the curator.
**Optional:** Tautulli for watch stats and purge suggestions; Fanart.tv for richer poster art. For Ollama on the Unraid host, set `LLM_PROVIDER=ollama` and `LLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1` (Extra Parameters already add `host.docker.internal`).
**Support:** [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/romwil/curatorx/issues) · [Unraid wiki](https://github.com/romwil/curatorx/blob/main/docs/wiki/Unraid.md)

Config.Name: Web UI port
Config.Description: CuratorX web interface (LAN only recommended)

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: settings.json, SQLite database, and auto-generated session secret

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Container timezone for preferred library sync hour (e.g. America/New_York)

Config.Name: PLEX_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: Plex server URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:32400)

Config.Name: PLEX_TOKEN
Config.Description: Optional seed: Plex Media Server token for library access (not household Sign in with Plex)

Config.Name: RADARR_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: Radarr URL

Config.Name: RADARR_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: Radarr API key

Config.Name: SONARR_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: Sonarr URL

Config.Name: SONARR_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: Sonarr API key

Config.Name: TMDB_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: TMDB API key (metadata + discovery)

Config.Name: FANART_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: Fanart.tv API key for richer poster art

Config.Name: TAUTULLI_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: Tautulli URL for watch stats / purge suggestions

Config.Name: TAUTULLI_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: Tautulli API key

Config.Name: LLM_PROVIDER
Config.Description: LLM provider: openai_compatible, anthropic, ollama (or other presets in Settings)

Config.Name: LLM_BASE_URL
Config.Description: Optional seed: LLM base URL (e.g. http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 for Ollama)

Config.Name: LLM_API_KEY
Config.Description: Optional seed: LLM API key (not required for Ollama)

Config.Name: LLM_MODEL
Config.Description: Optional seed: chat model name

Config.Name: CURATORX_SESSION_SECRET
Config.Description: Optional: strong secret for multi-user session cookies (auto-generated under Config if unset)

Config.Name: CURATORX_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Config.Description: Secret for Plex webhook auth header X-CuratorX-Webhook-Secret (required if using webhooks)

Config.Name: CURATORX_MCP_API_KEY
Config.Description: Privacy MCP key for HTTP /mcp — public schema, read-only. Leave empty to generate in Admin → Advanced.

Config.Name: CURATORX_MCP_FULL_API_KEY
Config.Description: Full MCP key for trusted in-stack /mcp (internal fields + confirm-gated *arr). Must differ from the privacy key. Or generate in Admin → Advanced.
